title: Residential Support Worker
description:
We are seeking experienced Youth Residential Support Workers to provide care and safeguarding for young people in mainstream residential settings.

This is a flexible, temporary role with varied shifts including earlies, lates, sleep-ins and waking nights. You will support young people in care, helping them build independence and resilience while managing complex needs.

Responsibilities include delivering person-centred support, maintaining care records, assisting with routines and education, and following safeguarding procedures. Experience in residential childcare or youth support is required.
summary: Flexible residential role supporting young people in care with strong hourly pay and varied shifts.

title: Residential Childcare Support Worker
description:
This full-time Residential Childcare Support Worker role offers 35 hours per week plus sleep-in shifts within a structured care environment in Leeds.

You will support young people in residential settings, helping them develop independence, stability and life skills. The role includes working as part of a team, maintaining safe environments and contributing to care plans.

Ideal for candidates seeking a stable, long-term position with additional earning potential through sleep-in shifts.
summary: Full-time residential childcare role with sleep-in earnings and stable Leeds-based placement.

title: Youth Residential Support Worker
description:
This flexible Youth Residential Support Worker role focuses on supporting young people aged 12–17 in mainstream residential homes.

You will provide stability, promote independence and create a safe, supportive environment. The role includes working varied shifts and supporting individuals with behavioural or emotional needs.

Previous experience in residential childcare or similar settings is required, along with strong safeguarding awareness.
summary: Flexible youth support role in Leeds with strong pay and meaningful impact in residential care.

title: Behaviour Support Worker
company: Leeds Supply Service Ltd • Agency • Temporary
description:
Behaviour Support Workers are required to support pupils in mainstream and specialist education settings across Leeds.

You will work alongside teaching staff, supporting individuals and small groups with behavioural and emotional needs. The role involves assisting with learning, promoting engagement and helping students progress.

Suitable for candidates with experience in behaviour support or education-based support roles.
summary: Daily-rate role supporting pupils with behavioural needs across Leeds schools.

title: Relief Extra Care Support Worker
description:
Join a supportive team providing extra care services to older adults in a Leeds-based setting.

You will assist individuals with daily living, wellbeing and social engagement while promoting independence and dignity. Duties include personal support, domestic tasks and helping service users access community activities.

This role offers flexibility, training opportunities and a supportive working environment.
summary: Flexible relief role supporting older adults with strong training and supportive team environment.

title: Residential Childcare Support Worker
description:
This casual Residential Childcare Support Worker role offers flexible shift coverage within Leeds-based services.

You will provide support to young people in residential care, ensuring safe environments and contributing to their development and wellbeing.

Ideal for candidates seeking flexible work patterns while maintaining involvement in childcare support roles.
summary: Casual childcare support role offering flexible shifts across Leeds residential services.

title: Assistant Residential Support Worker
description:
Assistant Residential Support Workers are needed to support adults with mental health needs and learning disabilities in a structured residential setting.

You will help individuals maintain independence, support daily routines and contribute to positive outcomes. The role involves working flexible rotas including sleep-ins and weekends.

Previous experience in residential care is essential.
summary: Residential support role in Halifax focused on adult care with structured rota and stable placement.

title: Complex Care Assistant
description:
Provide complex care support to individuals in Brighouse, delivering high-quality assistance tailored to specific client needs.

You will support daily living, ensure safety and work closely with care plans to deliver consistent care. A driving licence and access to a vehicle are required.

This role offers strong hourly pay and progression opportunities.
summary: Higher-paying complex care role requiring driving, focused on personalised client support.

title: Complex Care Assistant
description:
Deliver complex care support in Huddersfield, working with clients who require tailored assistance.

You will provide consistent, high-quality care, supporting independence and wellbeing while following care plans. Driving is required for this role.

Suitable for candidates seeking well-paid care roles with responsibility.
summary: Well-paid complex care role in Huddersfield with strong hourly rate and client-focused work.

title: Nights Health Care Assistant
description:
This night shift Health Care Assistant role supports residents within a care home environment in Castleford.

You will assist with overnight care, ensuring safety, comfort and wellbeing for residents. The role includes both full-time and part-time opportunities with structured shift patterns.

Ideal for candidates seeking stable night work in a care setting.
summary: Night shift care role offering stable hours within a structured residential environment.

title: Care Assistant
description:
Join a care team delivering home-based support in Wakefield, helping individuals maintain independence and quality of life.

You will provide personalised care, assist with daily activities and offer companionship. Full training is provided, making this suitable for both experienced and new carers.

A rewarding role focused on meaningful, community-based care.
summary: Home-based care role in Wakefield with training provided and strong earning potential.
